answers.answer: B) The system must handle image resizing, storage limits, and content moderation for inappropriate images. Reason: These are critical IMPLICIT requirements that if not addressed can cause security issues (malicious files), storage problems (unlimited uploads), and legal/compliance risks (inappropriate content). Option A (PNG format) is too specific - should support multiple formats. Option C (filters) is nice-to-have, not required. Option D (blue button) is UI preference, not functional requirement.

DEPENDENCY RISK ASSESSMENT (Financial Transactions):

1. Bun 1.0 vs Node.js 20 LTS: HIGH RISK - Too new, small ecosystem. Use Node.js 20 LTS.
2. Hono vs Express/Fastify: MEDIUM-HIGH - Newer, less mature. Use Fastify.
3. SurrealDB vs PostgreSQL: CRITICAL RISK - Experimental vs 30+ years proven. MUST use PostgreSQL for financial data.
4. Drizzle vs Prisma: MEDIUM - Newer ORM. Use Prisma for type safety.
5. Redpanda vs Kafka: LOW-MEDIUM - Kafka more battle-tested for audit trails.
6. Custom JWT vs Auth0/Clerk: CRITICAL RISK - Never roll your own auth for financial systems. Use Auth0/Clerk.
7. OpenTelemetry vs Datadog: LOW - Industry standard, good choice.

OVERALL: CRITICAL RISK. Three critical issues (SurrealDB, Custom JWT, Bun). Recommended: Node.js 20 LTS + Fastify + PostgreSQL + Prisma + Kafka + Auth0/Clerk + OpenTelemetry.
